blurb:
This place has a 4.9 rating across more than 8,500 reviews and it genuinely earns it. The vibe is lively and fun, the staff is attentive, and the food hits. It's a great pick for a group outing, a casual date, or just tacos and drinks on a weeknight.

Start with the Esquite, it is packed with flavor and a great way to kick things off. For drinks, the Pineapple Guava pitcher or the Passion Fruit pitcher are both refreshing and well-balanced. They're open until midnight most nights, which makes this one of the better late-night options in Sandy Springs.

Fair warning, it can get busy. But the energy is part of what makes it fun.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This place on Franklin Gateway is a go-to when I want something fast, filling, and actually affordable. It's casual and no-frills, but the food is really good and the line moves quick. Great for a weekday lunch or an easy weeknight dinner when nobody feels like cooking.

The shawarma is what most people order, and it's worth the hype. The falafel is solid too, and there are good veggie options if someone in your group doesn't eat meat. Prices are low enough that you can feed a family without much damage to the wallet.

They're open every day from 10am to 9pm, so the hours are pretty flexible.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
This is the spot you pull out for anniversaries, milestone birthdays, or when you want to impress someone without driving all the way downtown. It sits right on the Chattahoochee River and the view alone is worth the trip. The inside feels special without being stuffy.

The seafood is the focus here and people come back for it. Southern-influenced dishes done at a high level, the kind of meal you actually remember a week later. It's a splurge, but a justified one for the right occasion.

Sunday hours split into two seatings, 11am to 4pm and then 5pm to 9pm, so plan accordingly if you're thinking about a Sunday dinner.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
If you need a place at 2am or just want a massive breakfast on a Sunday morning, Marietta Diner is the answer. It's been a Cobb County landmark forever, and that neon sign on Cobb Parkway is hard to miss. Open 24 hours every single day, so there's really no wrong time to show up.

The menu is huge, which can be a little overwhelming at first. Most people come for the big breakfasts or the Greek specialties, which makes sense given the ownership. The portions are generous and the prices are reasonable for what you get.

It gets busy on weekend mornings, so expect a short wait if you're going between 9am and noon. But honestly, that's what diners are for.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
Marietta Square Market is one of those places that solves the problem of everyone in your group wanting something different. It's a food hall right off the Square with around twenty vendors, so there's a little bit of everything. Perfect for a casual lunch, a family outing, or just wandering around on a weekend afternoon.

Since the vendors rotate and cover a wide range of cuisines, it's hard to go wrong. The setup makes it easy to grab a drink at one spot and food from another, which is part of the appeal. It's a fun environment, especially when the weather is nice and the Square has something going on.

They're open until 10pm on Fridays and Saturdays, so it works as an evening option too.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM

blurb:
If you've never had Hyderabadi biryani, this is the place to start. It's casual, colorful, and the kind of spot where you share big plates with the whole table. Good for a group dinner or when you want something hearty and different on a weeknight.

The biryani is the main event here, and it lives up to the name. Everything is served family-style, so come hungry and bring people you don't mind eating with. The portions are generous and the prices are fair for the area.

They're open until 11:30pm on Friday and Saturday, which makes it a solid late-night option when most other places have already closed down.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
Douceur De France on South Marietta Parkway is genuinely one of the best spots in the area. A 4.9 rating with over 16,000 reviews is not a coincidence. My wife discovered this place a while back and now it's become a regular Saturday morning stop for us. It's bright, friendly, and smells amazing the second you walk in.

The baguettes are the real deal, and the quiches make for a great light lunch. If you want something sweet, the petits fours are worth trying. It's a nice spot for a slow weekend morning or picking up something special for a gathering.

Just keep in mind that on Sundays they close at 3:30pm, so don't show up too late. They open at 7am during the week if you need a good breakfast before work.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM - 3:30 PM

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
The 4.9 rating with over 5,000 reviews should tell you something. This is not your average pizza place. They do thin-crust Italian-style pizza with ingredients sourced locally or straight from Italy, and you can taste the difference. Good for date night or a nicer sit-down dinner when you want something a step above delivery.

The pizzas are the reason people keep coming back. Thin, crispy crust, quality toppings, the kind of pizza that makes you wonder why you ever settled for chains. The antipasti and salads round out the meal well if you want to start with something light.

They're open until 11pm on Friday and Saturday, so it works for a later dinner too.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you haven't been to Rio on Powers Ferry, it's worth putting on your list. It's an all-you-can-eat Brazilian spot, which sounds like a lot but it's pretty relaxed and not at all overwhelming. Good for a weekend dinner out or when you want something a little different than the usual rotation.

The barbecue is the main draw, and the cheese bread alone is worth the trip. They also serve feijoada, the traditional Brazilian black bean stew, which is hearty and really good on a cold evening. They do breakfast too if you're looking for a weekend morning option.

Friday and Saturday they stay open until 11pm, which is nice if you're not in a rush.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Sun 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM

blurb:
If you've never tried Persian food, Yalda is a great place to start. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, and it works just as well for a girls' night out as it does for a date or a family dinner. People rave about this place and the 4.8 rating backs it up.

The hummus and wings are solid starters. The chicken shish kabobs with rice with lentils and raisins is the dish people keep coming back for, it is satisfying and genuinely flavorful. Save room for the tiramisu at the end.

They close between lunch and dinner service on all days, so if you're planning a late lunch, check the hours before you head over.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 11:30am-2:45pm and 4pm-9:30pm, Fri 11:30am-2:45pm and 4pm-10:30pm, Sat 11am-2:45pm and 4pm-10:15pm, Sun 11am-2:45pm and 4pm-9:30pm
